name: Versioning & Unified bootstrap.sh

files_create.purpose: The GIP inheritance mechanism at the repo root (standard/GIP.md Section 2). POSIX-compatible bash; begins with `set -euo pipefail`. Configuration exclusively via GOVERNANCE_REPO_URL and a pinned GOVERNANCE_TAG (both overridable by environment variable or CLI flag) — never a floating branch reference such as 'main'. Implements, as one atomic operation: (1) clone the framework at the pinned GOVERNANCE_TAG into a `mktemp` temp directory (never operates in place first); (2) inject standard/, templates/, schema/, tools/, and NIZAM.json from the cloned tag into the consumer repo under a `.nizam/` governance directory by default, with the target directory configurable via flag/variable; (3) verify the injection before declaring success — NIZAM.json parses as valid JSON, all required governance files are present and non-empty, and every path indexed under the injected `.nizam/` tree resolves on disk; (4) write `.nizam/provenance.json` recording the pinned GOVERNANCE_TAG, an ISO-8601 timestamp, and the GOVERNANCE_REPO_URL source; (5) atomicity — all cloning and staging happens in the temp directory, the temp stage is moved into place under `.nizam/` only after verification passes, and `trap` handlers clean up the temp directory on any failure or early exit so a failed run never leaves a partial `.nizam/` in place. Also implements a `--verify-only` mode (per GIP.md Section 3/4 drift detection) that re-runs step 3's checks against an already-injected `.nizam/` directory without cloning or writing anything, and a `--help` mode that prints usage and exits 0. Both `--help` and `--verify-only` are pure local operations and MUST NOT invoke git clone or otherwise touch the network — network access is confined to the clone step of a full (non-flag) bootstrap run. Contains no NizamIQ-specific infrastructure endpoint strings (no 'nizamiq', no '.svc' / 'cluster.local' hostnames); GOVERNANCE_REPO_URL defaults to the generic nizam-framework repository reference only.

files_modify.purpose: Add the v0.1.0 genesis release entry, positioned in the file below (following) the existing '## [Unreleased]' heading, per Keep a Changelog convention (Unreleased stays topmost; dated releases are listed beneath it). The v0.1.0 entry carries a dated heading (e.g. '## [0.1.0] - 2026-07-07') and prose/bullet content describing the framework genesis — the six governance modules (standard/, methodology/, registry/, templates/, schema/, tools/) plus the root NIZAM.json index and bootstrap.sh shipped in this release. This is the sole contracted changelog authoring scope for the whole phase; no other feature's contract covers a CHANGELOG.md entry.

Orchestrator-sanctioned exception: mark phase '001-framework-genesis' status as 'complete' (from 'in_progress') now that this final feature (007) closes the phase, and refresh 'updated_at'. No other manifest fields change; no other docs/planning/ file is touched by this contract.

non_goals: No edits to any content within standard/, methodology/, registry/, templates/, schema/, or tools/ — those modules are shipped as-is and only referenced/copied by path from bootstrap.sh
No new governance modules and no changes to NIZAM.json's indexed content

No actual network operations performed during this contract's verification — the --help and --verify-only code paths under test are designed to require no network access; verification never invokes a real `git clone` of any remote URL
No CI/CD pipeline wiring, no release-tagging automation, and no actual creation of a git tag — bootstrap.sh reads a GOVERNANCE_TAG value, it does not create one
No changes to any contract, qa verdict, or run_state.json content beyond what the generator's Step 7 durable-state update performs after this contract is implemented
No modification of any file under .agent/contracts/, .agent/qa/, or .agent/evidence/ other than this contract file and this feature's own numbered evidence files

verification.acceptance_test: bootstrap.sh exists at the repository root and passes a bash syntax check
verification.command: test -f bootstrap.sh && bash -n bootstrap.sh && echo PASS || { echo "bootstrap.sh missing or fails bash -n"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: bootstrap.sh enables bash strict mode (set -euo pipefail)
verification.command: test -f bootstrap.sh || { echo 'MISSING bootstrap.sh'; exit 1; }; grep -q 'set -euo pipefail' bootstrap.sh && echo PASS || { echo 'strict mode line not found'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: bootstrap.sh's clone invocation uses the pinned GOVERNANCE_TAG variable on a non-comment line, and no non-comment line contains a floating 'main' branch reference (--branch=main, --branch main, checkout main, or refs/heads/main). Comment lines (first non-space character '#') are excluded from the scan so an illustrative anti-pattern comment cannot cause a false fail, and only precise ERE spellings of the floating-branch anti-patterns are rejected so an unrelated 'main' substring cannot cause a false pass.
verification.command: test -f bootstrap.sh || { echo 'MISSING bootstrap.sh'; exit 1; }; code_lines=$(grep -v '^[[:space:]]*#' bootstrap.sh); echo "$code_lines" | grep -Eq -- '--branch[= ]+"?\$\{?GOVERNANCE_TAG' || { echo 'no non-comment clone invocation using --branch with GOVERNANCE_TAG found'; exit 1; }; echo "$code_lines" | grep -Eq -- '--branch[= ]+main\b|checkout[[:space:]]+main\b|refs/heads/main' && { echo 'floating main-branch reference found on a non-comment line'; exit 1; } || echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: No NizamIQ infrastructure endpoint strings anywhere in bootstrap.sh (guarded absence check — refuses to trust an empty grep unless the file is confirmed present first)
verification.command: test -f bootstrap.sh || { echo 'MISSING bootstrap.sh, refusing to trust an absence-of-match result'; exit 1; }; if grep -Eiq 'nizamiq|\.svc|cluster\.local' bootstrap.sh; then echo FAIL; exit 1; else echo PASS; fi

verification.acceptance_test: bootstrap.sh injects standard/, templates/, and schema/ into the consumer target and includes an explicit verification step
verification.command: test -f bootstrap.sh || { echo 'MISSING bootstrap.sh'; exit 1; }; grep -q 'standard' bootstrap.sh && grep -q 'templates' bootstrap.sh && grep -q 'schema' bootstrap.sh && grep -Eiq 'verify' bootstrap.sh && echo PASS || { echo 'missing an injected module reference or a verify step'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: bootstrap.sh writes .nizam/provenance.json recording the pinned tag, a timestamp, and the source repo URL
verification.command: test -f bootstrap.sh || { echo 'MISSING bootstrap.sh'; exit 1; }; grep -q 'provenance.json' bootstrap.sh && grep -q 'GOVERNANCE_REPO_URL' bootstrap.sh && grep -Eiq 'timestamp|date' bootstrap.sh && echo PASS || { echo 'provenance recording incomplete'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: bootstrap.sh supports a --verify-only mode for drift detection
verification.command: test -f bootstrap.sh || { echo 'MISSING bootstrap.sh'; exit 1; }; grep -q -- '--verify-only' bootstrap.sh && echo PASS || { echo 'no --verify-only flag found'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: bootstrap.sh stages into a temp directory and only moves into place after verification, with a cleanup trap on failure (atomicity)
verification.command: test -f bootstrap.sh || { echo 'MISSING bootstrap.sh'; exit 1; }; grep -Eq 'mktemp' bootstrap.sh && grep -q 'trap' bootstrap.sh && echo PASS || { echo 'missing mktemp staging or trap-based cleanup' ; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: Functional smoke test: bash bootstrap.sh --help exits 0, prints usage, and requires no network access (a 5s timeout guards against an accidental network hang)
verification.command: test -f bootstrap.sh || { echo 'MISSING bootstrap.sh'; exit 1; }; output=$(timeout 5 bash bootstrap.sh --help 2>&1); status=$?; echo "$output"; test "$status" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "exit status $status (possible network hang or --help failure)"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: CHANGELOG.md contains a v0.1.0 genesis entry, positioned below (following, later in the file than) the existing '## [Unreleased]' heading, per Keep a Changelog convention
verification.command: test -f CHANGELOG.md || { echo 'MISSING CHANGELOG.md'; exit 1; }; grep -q '\[0.1.0\]' CHANGELOG.md || { echo 'no [0.1.0] heading found'; exit 1; }; grep -q '\[Unreleased\]' CHANGELOG.md || { echo 'no [Unreleased] heading found'; exit 1; }; v_line=$(grep -n '\[0.1.0\]' CHANGELOG.md | head -1 | cut -d: -f1); u_line=$(grep -n '\[Unreleased\]' CHANGELOG.md | head -1 | cut -d: -f1); test "$u_line" -lt "$v_line" && echo PASS || { echo "expected [Unreleased] (line $u_line) above [0.1.0] (line $v_line)"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: The v0.1.0 CHANGELOG entry describes the framework genesis (mentions the shipped governance modules or bootstrap.sh, not just a bare version heading)
verification.command: test -f CHANGELOG.md || { echo 'MISSING CHANGELOG.md'; exit 1; }; grep -Eiq 'genesis|standard/|methodology/|registry/|templates/|schema/|tools/|bootstrap' CHANGELOG.md && echo PASS || { echo 'v0.1.0 entry lacks genesis description content'; exit 1; }

test -f docs/planning/manifest.json || { echo 'MISSING docs/planning/manifest.json'; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import json
d = json.load(open('docs/planning/manifest.json'))
matches = [p for p in d.get('phases', []) if p.get('id') == '001-framework-genesis']
assert matches, 'phase 001-framework-genesis not found in manifest.json'
assert matches[0].get('status') == 'complete', f\"expected status complete, got {matches[0].get('status')}\"
print('PASS')
"

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/007-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/007-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/007-verify-12.txt for the twelfth). One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
